Yet the consequence arrived with a soft, inevitable weight: the earth remembered each vow. Crops stood taller, boats found favorable winds, neighbors forgave the broken, and the Gate, once silent, opened only to those who kept faith.

Thus the vow became law; keeping faith shaped life.
